patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "Hello?"] Hello.

[Clinician asked: "Hello there. It's uh Doctor  here. How can I help you this afternoon?"] Ohh, I just got a terrible headache since mid-day. Um on the left side. It's just making me feel so ill.  I just feel like I need to vomit.
[Clinician asked: "I'm sorry to hear that. Um can you tell me a bit more about the headache?"] Well you know, I noticed some zig-zag lines in my vision a few minutes before the headache started.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Mm-hmm."] My vision blurred. Um, you know, it just I, yeah. Pain's just horrible. Just making me feel so unwell. Um
[Clinician asked: "Mm, OK. And <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>"] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>  just want you to do something.

[Clinician asked: "Well, let's try our best. Let's try and get you, let's try and get you well, soon. Um you mentioned it's on your left hand side. Is that right? And it started at mid-day, a few hours ago."] Yeah.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um did the pain come on quite suddenly? Or was it more of a gradual build up?"] Don't know, really. It just happened.

[Clinician asked: "OK. And are you able to describe what kind of headache it was? For example, was it throbbing, or was it more of a sharp pain?"] Yeah, I guess it's, yeah I guess it's throbbing, um on that left side.
[Clinician asked: "And is it moving anywhere else, at all?"] No, but it's worse when I move.
[Clinician asked: "Uh OK. Is that when you move your neck?"] Well, like if I lean forward, or like move my head or neck, yeah, headache gets worse.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um"] I'm just really worried. Like I've just been googling and like I've read,  you know if your  headache gets worse if you move, it could be a sign of brain cancer.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Is that all you're worried about?"] cancer.

[Clinician asked: "Is that, is that all your main worries today?"] Ohh yeah, yeah and I'd like, you know My, my friend's mum, she she recently died of a brain tumor. So, it's really concerning me actually.

[Clinician asked: "Oh dear, I'm sorry to hear that. I can, I can understand why it's affecting you. Um But <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> why don't we, why don't we take a good history first, and let me examine you. And then we can have a little chat afterwards, and see what's going on here."] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>

[Clinician asked: "Um so you mentioned a few things. You mentioned a headache. You also mentioned some disease, like Lyme's, uh before the headaches came on. And your vision has been blurry as well. Is that right? Is that <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>, is that on both eyes, or just in left, left eye?"] Yeah. Eh, feels like it some days.
[Clinician asked: "Both eyes, OK. Have you any difficulty with seeing bright lights, at all?"] Yeah well I'm , wearing sunglasses right now. It's just, you know, the light's really hurting my eyes.
[Clinician asked: "Ohh, I see OK. Um and you mentioned that you felt quite nauseous. Have you vomited at all?"] Yeah. I vomited twice.
[Clinician asked: "Twice, OK.  Um  earlier today, OK. Um, any temperatures or fevers?"] Um, no I don't feel feverish.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um, any other funny skin rashes that you may have noticed?"] No, I haven't noticed anything like that.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um fine. Um, and do you normally get headaches?"] Um, I'm not really prone to them, no. No but I did, I did have a similar headache a few weeks ago.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And what happened then?"] Just  similar.
[Clinician asked: "How long did it last for?"] Couple of days.
[Clinician asked: "Did you do anything that made it better?"] Um yeah I had to take painkillers, and had to go go to bed and rest and
[Clinician asked: "Yeah."] couple days off work, and I work in the fashion industry. And you know, they're wondering what's going on, like I have to take sick days.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Yeah. Yeah."] I need to get back to work today. But you know, what if it is a brain tumour? What's gonna happen with my future and my career?
[Clinician asked: "Yeah. Yeah. <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>"] You know,  really  about this.  never had headaches like this before. Like why should I get them now?

[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Yeah, sure. I mean I can see your concern obviously is a lot, there's a lot of worry there. Um but you know let let let, as I said let's take it step by step, um to see exactly what the cause of these headaches are first. Um, I'm just going to ask you some, um very uh yes no ques"] No.
[Clinician asked: "Any problems with your arms and legs? For example numbness or weakness?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "Any difficulty with balance, your balance or coordination?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And have you had any injuries to your head? Have you had a fall recently, or been knocked on the head?"] No.

[Clinician asked: "OK, alright. Um and and you're feeling otherwise OK. So, for example your um Uh your your breathing's OK? You're not feeling breathless, or No, OK. Um do you have any other medical history I should be aware of?"] No, nothing.
[Clinician asked: "No. So for example, have you had, has anyone told you you've had migraines in the past?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "No, OK. Do you"] No. My mum, my mum has, has migraines. But I've, I've not been diagnosed.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Right,  my next question was that of family history. Is there anything in the family? So apart from migraines, is there anything else? Is there anything related to brain, for example?"] as well.
[Clinician asked: "What was that, sorry?"] My mom has an underactive thyroid as well.

[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> thyroid  as well. OK. Um OK any other problems with for example, the brain, or? Um you mentioned brain cancer. Any other family history of ?"] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> No.
[Clinician asked: "No, OK. Um in terms of medications, do you take anything on a regular basis?"] Um I take the pill, Microgynon.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And how long have you been on that for?"] three months.
[Clinician asked: "Three months, OK. And is that mainly for contraception, or is that for something else?"] it's for contraception.

[Clinician asked: "OK, alright. That's something you meant to have a little discussion about today as well. Um Uh well my next question was about um your social, your situation at home. You mentioned you work in the fashion industry. And your work, is your job quite stressful at the moment?"] Yeah it's really stressful, actually.
[Clinician asked: "Have you been"] having problems at like  work, you know managers like putting a lot of pressure on me to deliver and, uh yeah it's not a good time.
[Clinician asked: ". Is it getting you down?"] I don't feel down, just a little bit stressed.
[Clinician asked: "OK, alright. And what kind of things do you do to alleviate your stress, normally?"] Nothing really.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Do you do much in the way of exercise?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "No, OK. Um who's at home with you?"] Um, I live on my own.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um are you coping OK at the moment?"] Fine.
[Clinician asked: "Yeah? OK. Um do you smoke at all? <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> and do you drink much in the way of alcohol?"] No. No.

[Clinician asked: "OK, alright. Um, so normally at this  I'd like to kind of proceed to examination. Um That's um <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> based on your story, um I wonder whether you may, uh may have a migraine, uh  migraine type headache. Uh it sounds Um, very classical of that, of that nature, the the the  headache on o"] thank you,  uh thanks so much. I was so worried that I had cancer. And, ohh now you say migraine. Yeah I guess my mum has similar symptoms to me and yeah, it does make sense.

[Clinician asked: "yeah. Yeah. Well, I hope that puts your, you know,  mind at ease. Um, certainly this is the second migraine you've had in the space of a month. Um, So I think, moving forwards, there's a couple of options. I think keeping a headache diary would be quite important, uh to see exactly what's triggering"] K that sounds good.

[Clinician asked: "Um and uh, you know if your headaches were to become more persistent, then we could maybe think about giving you some medications that will stop your headaches from happening in the first place, something  prophylactic medicines. then we could have a discussion next time I see you next week, perhaps"] OK. OK.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Um, now a couple of things I want you to think about is, you know if your symptoms aren't getting better next two three days, or indeed they're getting worse. I'd like you to come back and see me, um straight away if possible. So if your headache's getting worse, not responding to medications Or"] OK.
[Clinician asked: "Does that sound  all OK?"] Doctor, that's great.
[Clinician asked: "Great. Any questions for me?"] No. Thank you.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Have a good day!"] K thanks. Bye!
gold_note:
Headache on left side. Started few hours ago, gradual onset. Throbbing in nature. Nil radiating. 
Had visual aura before onset of headaches - zigzag lines in field of vision. Also associated photophobia and blurred vision. Feels nauseous and has vomited twice today. Also reports some neck pain. Nil neck stiffness. 
No speech disturbances
No limb weakness or numbness
No coordination/balance issues
No recent head injury 
No fever
No rashes 

Similar episode 2/52 ago - resolved with analgesia. Pt worried about brain Ca. 

PMH: nil
DH: microgynon 
NKDA
FH: mother - migraine 
SH: lives alone, works in fashion industry. Feeling stressed ++. Nil mood sx. Nil smoking/etOH hx

Imp: migraine

Plan:
1.	Change COCP to POP
2.	Keep headache diary 
3.	Regular paracetamol/NSAIDs
4.	Review in 1w to discuss prophylactic Rx, or earlier if worsening sx (headaches, vomiting, vision sx) or any concerns

gold_sections.HIGHLIGHTS:
Headache
Throbbing
visual aura
photophobia
microgynon
migraine

naive.response_text:
I understand how worried you must be, especially given your recent experience and the loss of your friend's mum. It's completely natural to feel concerned when symptoms like these arise. However, based on what you've described—headache on one side, visual disturbances, nausea, and sensitivity to light—it sounds like a classic presentation of a migraine. Migraines can indeed cause such symptoms, and the fact that your mother has a history of migraines might explain this pattern.

I want to reassure you that I'll conduct a thorough evaluation to rule out any other serious causes. For now, let's focus on managing your symptoms. Keeping a diary to track your headaches could help identify triggers. If your symptoms worsen or don't improve, please come back for further assessment. You're not alone in this, and we'll work together to find the best approach for you.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing all of this with me. I can understand how worried you must be, especially given your friend’s mum’s experience and your own symptoms. It’s completely reasonable to be concerned about serious causes like brain cancer, especially with symptoms like headaches that worsen with movement and visual disturbances.

From what you’ve described, we’ll start by evaluating the most likely causes of your symptoms. Migraines are a possibility, especially since your mother has a history of them, and they can present with throbbing pain, nausea, vomiting, and visual changes like zig-zag lines or blurring [PMID ?]. Another consideration is tension headaches, though they typically don’t cause visual disturbances. We’ll also need to rule out other causes like infections or neurological issues, though these are less likely given your history and symptoms so far.

To move forward, I’ll perform a neurological examination to check for any signs that might indicate a more serious condition. Depending on the findings, we may consider imaging, like a CT scan, to ensure there’s no swelling or other changes in your brain [PMID ?]. In the meantime, I recommend keeping a headache diary to track when your headaches occur, what they feel like, and any potential triggers, such as stress, certain foods, or lack of sleep.

If your symptoms don’t improve within the next 2-3 days or if they get worse, please come back to see me as soon as possible. We can also discuss options for managing migraines if this pattern continues, such as preventive medications or lifestyle changes.

Before you leave, could you summarize what we’ve agreed on today? That way, I can make sure everything is clear to you.

full.response_text:
I can hear how much this is worrying you — you mentioned, "cancer" and also shared that your friend's mum recently passed away from a brain tumor. I want to take this seriously and make sure we get to the bottom of what's going on.

Your terrible headache, especially with symptoms like vision changes and vomiting, is concerning. Given your family history and the sudden onset, we need to check for serious causes like brain tumors. The guidelines suggest prioritizing imaging to rule out such possibilities.

Given your symptoms and family history, we'll prioritize an MRI to check for any concerning findings.

We'll also review your recent similar headache to see if there's a pattern.

If the imaging shows anything, we'll follow up immediately. Otherwise, we'll plan to see you again in 2 weeks to review the results.
